entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: A large seeded pulse legume, Cicer arietinum L.

entities.name: CICER-RHIZOBIUM
entities.description: A specific group of Rhizobium strains that nodulate Cicer spp.

entities.name: NODULATION
entities.description: The process of forming nodules on plant roots by Rhizobium strains.

entities.name: COTYLEDON REMOVAL
entities.description: The removal of cotyledons just after germination to induce dwarfed growth.

entities.name: DWARFISM
entities.description: Reduced seedling growth resulting from the removal of cotyledons.

entities.name: AXENIC CONDITIONS
entities.description: Sterile conditions used for growing plants in test tubes.

entities.name: ROOT MEDIUM
entities.description: Sand or sand + vermiculite used as the growth medium for roots.

entities.name: TRAP HOST
entities.description: Dwarfed chickpea seedlings used for counting and studying Cicer-Rhizobium.

entities.name: MOST PROBABLE NUMBER SERIAL DILUTION-PLANT INFECTION TECHNIQUE
entities.description: A technique used for counting Cicer-Rhizobium in pure cultures and contaminated materials.

entities.name: RHIZOBIUM ECOLOGY
entities.description: The study of the relationship between Cicer-Rhizobium and its environment.

entities.name: STRAIN AUTHENTICATION
entities.description: The process of verifying the identity of a specific Rhizobium strain.

entities.name: INOCULUM QUALITY CONTROL
entities.description: The process of ensuring the quality and purity of the inoculum.

relationships.source: MOST PROBABLE NUMBER SERIAL DILUTION-PLANT INFECTION TECHNIQUE
